summary: Strong SRE framing and truthful content, but contains a critical fabricated date range (Jun 2025 – Apr 2026 is in the future), missing key JD technologies (Java, Spring Boot, messaging queues), and a few minor consistency issues.

issues.issue: Employment dates are in the FUTURE — Jun 2025 to Apr 2026 cannot be a past role. Candidate's master brief shows Jan 2025 – May 2025 (Intern) then Jun 2025 onwards (full-time, current as of creation date). If this resume was generated in early 2025, the full-time role should show 'Jun 2025 – Present' or the actual current month, NOT a future end date.
issues.where: Professional Experience > Site Reliability Engineer (DevOps Engineer) > Jun 2025 – Apr 2026
issues.suggestion: Change to 'Jun 2025 – Present' if the candidate is currently employed, or use the actual last month worked if they have left. Never use future dates for past roles.

issues.issue: The master brief states microservices count as '10+' (corrected from 20+), but the original cv.md supplied by the user still says '20+ services' in the summary. The generated resume correctly uses '10+ microservices' in multiple places, which matches the truthfulness anchor. However, verify this is the intended correction and consistent across all materials.
issues.where: Multiple locations (summary, experience bullets) state '10+ microservices'
issues.suggestion: Confirm the '10+' figure is the correct, defensible number per the master brief correction note. If so, no change needed here — this is correct. Flag only to ensure alignment with the brief's '(corrected from 20+)' note.

issues.issue: Job description likely emphasizes backend application technologies (Java, Spring Boot, microservices frameworks, message queues like Kafka/RabbitMQ, SQL/NoSQL databases) that are missing or under-represented. The resume focuses heavily on infrastructure/platform SRE but lacks mention of application-layer reliability, APM, or backend service ownership.
issues.where: Technical Skills section and experience bullets
issues.suggestion: If the JD mentions Java/Spring Boot, Kafka, RabbitMQ, or application performance monitoring, add a skills row like 'Application Technologies & APM: RDS (PostgreSQL), API Gateway, AWS X-Ray, Lambda' and weave application-layer observability or service ownership into a bullet (e.g., 'monitored application-layer SLIs for RESTful microservices').

issues.issue: SRE-I roles often call out 'on-call rotation,' 'incident management,' 'alerting,' 'SLI/SLO/SLA,' and 'toil automation' explicitly. The resume mentions runbooks, post-mortems, SLO monitoring, and toil reduction, but does not use the exact phrases 'on-call' or 'incident management' which are common ATS keywords.
issues.suggestion: Add 'on-call' to a bullet (e.g., 'supported on-call rotation for production incidents') and consider 'Incident Management' as a skill-row item or inline keyword.

issues.issue: Role title uses parenthetical '(DevOps Engineer)' which may confuse ATS parsers or readers — the official title per the brief is 'DevOps Engineer,' and the SRE framing is a narrative choice, not a dual title.
issues.where: Professional Experience > Site Reliability Engineer (DevOps Engineer)
issues.suggestion: Use either 'DevOps Engineer' (official title) or 'Site Reliability Engineer' (SRE-framed) — not both in parentheses. Recommend 'DevOps Engineer' as the title line, then lead bullets with SRE-framed verbs (Sustained, Drove) to convey the role focus.

issues.issue: Company name 'eSparkBiz' appears without 'Technologies' in experience section, but master brief and cv.md both show 'eSparkBiz Technologies.' Maintain full legal name for consistency.
issues.suggestion: Change to 'eSparkBiz Technologies · Ahmedabad, India' to match source materials.

issues.issue: Summary does not mention 'incident response' or 'on-call' explicitly, which are core SRE-I responsibilities and high-value keywords for this role.
issues.suggestion: Adjust summary to include 'incident response' or 'on-call,' e.g., '...driving incident response, on-call readiness, SLO monitoring, and ~70% toil reduction...'

issues.issue: Tech stack line for CRM project is very long (11 tools listed) and may wrap awkwardly at 9.5pt italic. Consider shortening to the 6-8 most relevant for SRE (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, EKS, Terraform, ArgoCD, Ingress-NGINX).
issues.where: Projects > Cloud-Native SaaS CRM Platform tech line
issues.suggestion: Trim to: 'Terraform · AWS EKS · ArgoCD · Ingress-NGINX · Prometheus · Grafana · Loki · Velero' (8 items) to keep one line and emphasize observability/reliability tools.

issues.issue: Bullet 'Provisioned multi-environment AWS infrastructure...' focuses on IaC speed/drift metrics but does not tie to SRE outcomes (uptime, incident reduction, deployment safety). For an SRE-I role, reframe to reliability impact.
issues.where: Experience > Site Reliability Engineer > last bullet
issues.suggestion: Reframe: 'Provisioned multi-environment AWS infrastructure on Linux using Terraform IaC (34 modules, S3 remote state) — enabling safe, repeatable deployments that sustained 99.9% uptime and reduced provisioning time 80% across Dev, Staging, Production.'
